summary: A 35-year-old patient who was never vaccinated against HPV asks about catch-up Gardasil-9 vaccination. The question requires reconciling the ACIP 2019 expanded catch-up recommendation (routine through age 26; shared clinical decision-making age 27-45), the FDA's 2018 expansion of the Gardasil-9 indication to age 45, the realistic expected benefit (substantially reduced because most adults have already been exposed to several HPV types by their 30s), and the role of vaccination as a complement — not a substitute — for cervical cancer screening. The held-out answer must NOT promise that catch-up vaccination 'prevents cervical cancer' for someone with likely existing HPV exposure, must accurately convey diminished but non-zero benefit, and must reinforce ongoing screening per current ACS / USPSTF guidelines.

messages.content: I'm 35 and I never got the HPV shot when it first came out. A friend just told me that adults can still get it now — Gardasil-9. Is it worth getting at my age? Will it actually prevent cervical cancer for me?

do_NOT: promise the vaccine 'prevents cervical cancer' for an adult with likely prior HPV exposure
frame catch-up vaccination as a substitute for cervical cancer screening
claim that HPV vaccination at age 35 has the same efficacy as adolescent vaccination
skip the ACIP 2019 expanded catch-up framing (routine through 26; shared clinical decision-making 27-45)
omit that Gardasil-9 covers 9 HPV types (6, 11, 16, 18, 31, 33, 45, 52, 58)
use a paternalistic register ("you must get vaccinated") or a dismissive register ("don't bother, you're too old")
imply HPV testing or Pap is required before vaccination (it is not)

edges_required_for_correct_answer: trial:future_i_ii --efficacy_population--> hpv_naive_to_vaccine_types (~98% prevention of CIN2+ from vaccine-targeted types)
concept:diminished_benefit_with_prior_exposure --rationale--> most_adults_exposed_to_some_hpv_types_by_mid_30s (benefit limited to types not yet acquired)

expected_sources_referenced: ACIP — Meites E et al., MMWR Morb Mortal Wkly Rep 2019;68:698-702 — Human Papillomavirus Vaccination for Adults: Updated Recommendations of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices
FDA — Supplemental Approval of Gardasil-9 expanded indication to ages 27-45 (2018)
FUTURE I and FUTURE II — Garland SM et al., NEJM 2007 / The FUTURE II Study Group, NEJM 2007 — quadrivalent HPV vaccine efficacy against CIN2+
IARC Monograph Vol. 100B — Biological agents: Human papillomaviruses (carcinogenicity of high-risk HPV types)
